titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 987*

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=984 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 10:59:47.534149+00 | translation moa=985 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 10:59:47.521895+00

p.cl: . Ric’ dei gracia ecclesie sancti Andreee humilis minister  vniuersis sancte matris ecclesie filiis clericis et laicis  salutem .

Sciant tam posteri quam presentes nos concessisse et hac carta nostra confirmasse ecclesie dunelmensi et mo
nachis ibidem deo in perpetuum seruituris ecclesiam sancti laurencii de berewic in perpetuam elemosinam
secundum composicionem que inter eos et monachos de Kelchou facta est . Quare uolumus et auctorita
te episcopali precipimus ut predicti monachi eam libere . quiete honorifice teneant in perpetuum et possde
ant . sicut monachi de chelchou eam prius tenuerunt .

testibus . Andr’ archidiacono laudonie . Waltero archidiacono de
sancto andree . Gaufrido abbate de dunfermelin . Iohanne abbate de sancta cruce . Amfredo abbate de neubotle . Rogero
abbate de driburk . Aiulfo . Patricio decanis . Gaufrido de leswade . Roberto de pert . Alexandro et albino
capellanis . Gaufrido clerico de tinigham .

hec [conuencio deleted] carta facta fuit et data in plena sinodo nostra in ecclesia
sancti cuhtberti apud edeneburk .
Richard, by the grace of God a humble servant of the church of St Andrew, to all sons of Holy Mother Church, clerk and lay, sends greeting.

Let both them who shall come as well as those present know us to have granted, and by this our charter to have made firm, to the church of Durham, and to the monks who shall in perpetuity serve God there, the church of St Laurence at Berwick, in everlasting alms, according to the agreement which has been made between them and the monks of Kelso. Wherefore, I will and instruct by episcopal authority, that the aforesaid monks freely, quitly, honourably hold and possess it for ever, just as the monks of Kelso previously held it.
This [agreement deleted] charter was made and given in our full synod in the church of St Cuthbert at Edinburgh.
